meta.title: Abstract & Spatial Reasoning Test
meta.description: 500 objective puzzles covering visual patterns, sequences, transformations, spatial relationships, and closely related reasoning skills.

questions.prompt: How many lines of symmetry does this ideal shape have?
questions.explanation: ○ (circle) has infinitely many line(s) of symmetry.

questions.prompt: How many lines of symmetry does this ideal shape have?
questions.explanation: □ (square) has 4 line(s) of symmetry.

questions.prompt: How many lines of symmetry does this ideal shape have?
questions.explanation: △ (equilateral triangle) has 3 line(s) of symmetry.

questions.prompt: How many lines of symmetry does this ideal shape have?
questions.explanation: ▭ (non-square rectangle) has 2 line(s) of symmetry.

questions.prompt: How many lines of symmetry does this ideal shape have?
questions.explanation: ◇ (non-square rhombus) has 2 line(s) of symmetry.

questions.prompt: How many lines of symmetry does this ideal shape have?
questions.explanation: ⬠ (regular pentagon) has 5 line(s) of symmetry.

questions.explanation: A decagonal pyramid has 11 faces, all of which appear in its net.

questions.prompt: How many flat faces must a complete net of a triangular antiprism contain?
questions.explanation: A triangular antiprism has 8 faces, all of which appear in its net.

questions.prompt: How many flat faces must a complete net of a cuboctahedron contain?
questions.explanation: A cuboctahedron has 14 faces, all of which appear in its net.

questions.prompt: How many flat faces must a complete net of a truncated tetrahedron contain?
questions.explanation: A truncated tetrahedron has 8 faces, all of which appear in its net.

questions.prompt: How many flat faces must a complete net of a truncated cube contain?
questions.explanation: A truncated cube has 14 faces, all of which appear in its net.
